title: Romantic Yeasty Malaysian Dill Bao
description: A soft, fluffy bao filled with the freshness of dill and the richness of yeast, perfect for a romantic dinner or special occasion.

ingredients.name: gluten-free all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: make sure to use a gluten-free flour blend for a tender crumb

ingredients.name: active dry yeast
ingredients.notes: use instant yeast for a faster fermentation time if desired

ingredients.name: warm water
ingredients.notes: not hot water, as yeast can die at high temperatures

ingredients.name: sugar
ingredients.notes: helps feed the yeast during fermentation

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: balances the sweetness and enhances the flavor of the dill

ingredients.name: fresh dill
ingredients.notes: chopped fresh dill adds a bright, fresh flavor to the bao

ingredients.name: sesame oil
ingredients.notes: add a nutty, slightly sweet flavor to the bao

ingredients.name: cornstarch
ingredients.notes: helps thicken the bao dough and gives it a tender texture

ingredients.name: egg
ingredients.notes: used for egg wash, adds a rich, golden color to the bao

instructions: In a small bowl, combine warm water, sugar, and yeast. Let it sit for 5 minutes until the yeast is activated and foamy.
In a large mixing bowl, combine gluten-free flour, cornstarch, and salt. Mix well to combine.
Add the yeast mixture to the flour mixture and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Preheat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal pieces, depending on how large you want your bao to be.
Roll each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly into a disk shape.
Place a tablespoon of sesame oil on each disk, then top with chopped fresh dill.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the bao.
Place the bao in the preheated wok and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and cooked through.
Brush with egg wash and serve immediately.
